reasoning: While the Trump administration and DOGE (Elon Musk, Vivek Ramaswamy) have a strong stated intent to massively reduce the federal workforce, structural realities make a >250k net reduction within a single calendar year highly challenging. Broad scale firings will face immediate injunctions, OPM procedural delays, and civil service protections. A strict hiring freeze combined with strict Return-to-Office mandates could drive large-scale attrition, but achieving a 250k drop requires freezing critical, high-turnover sectors like VA, DoD, and DHS, which is politically difficult. Betting markets' prior of 46% likely overestimates the speed at which government can be downsized due to comparisons with private sector sweeps (e.g., Elon's Twitter cuts). Thus, a more conservative 30% reflects the balance between intent and implementation limits.
